title: discover the amazing rice terraces of Mu cang Chai in Vietnam #rice. #vietnam #mucangchai #shorts
creator: Alex and Coni
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (agricultural/geometric-pattern landscapes -- rice terraces) -- not the pack's original single worked example (that role is bbc-earth-aerial.json, per spec AC3.4), but built to the exact same rigor/honesty standard. Independently re-verified this session (not merely trusted from the prior-agent candidate JSON) via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/8ZsiKtPixks&format=json), which returned title="discover the amazing rice terraces of Mu cang Chai in Vietnam #rice. #vietnam #mucangchai #shorts", author_name="Alex and Coni", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@alexandconi", and player height=200/width=113 -- an exact match to the title/creator fields in the candidate record, confirming this source_url in this session rather than trusting the prior pass verbatim. The 113x200 (portrait) oEmbed player aspect ratio, combined with the /shorts/ URL path, is consistent with a native vertical YouTube Shorts upload (the same structural signal used in this pack's the-nature-project-horseshoe-bend.json record); this is a hashtag-driven single fast aerial pass over the named Mu Cang Chai rice terraces in Vietnam, consistent with a travel-couple creator account rather than a broadcaster or contest submission. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': this session fetched the raw watch-page HTML for this URL directly (curl with a desktop User-Agent, HTTP 200, 1,379,158 bytes) and grepped the full response case-insensitively for 'creative commons', finding zero matches -- i.e. no explicit CC-license marking was found on this specific upload, so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. This session also attempted to extract a shortDescription field from the same watch-page HTML (grep for "shortDescription":") and a subscriberCountText field from a separate fetch of the creator's channel page (https://www.youtube.com/@alexandconi, HTTP 200, 1,198,503 bytes) as weak polish/reach signals -- both greps returned zero matches (this data is populated client-side by JavaScript and is not present in the raw HTML returned to a plain curl fetch), and a grep for DJI/Mavic/"drone model" mentions and genuine 4K/resolution disclosure similarly returned no real matches (the only '4k' hits were an unrelated YouTube experiment-flag string, 'disable_lifa_in_2k_4k', not a stated camera resolution) -- these null results are disclosed rather than papered over with an invented number or spec. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field and no YouTube Data API call was made this session -- rather than infer a number from the Shorts sub-60s convention, this field is left unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is ordinary travel-couple creator content (a single named-location aerial clip with genre hashtags), not a documented award, festival selection, or press-featured showcase -- inventing a provenance string here would violate the no-placeholders constitution rule. For context (disclosed, not independently re-verified in this session): the prior research pass reported considering two related terraces candidates and rejecting both in favor of this one -- a Banaue (Philippines) entry dropped for a noted restaurant-segment caveat, and a generic Vietnam rice-paddies entry dropped as geographically redundant with this Mu Cang Chai pick; this session did not re-run that comparison and takes no position on it beyond relaying it as background. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments, calibrated toward the low end of this pack's existing 0.72-0.88 range (sharpness=0.73, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.74): like this pack's other unvetted personal-creator exemplars (the-nature-project-horseshoe-bend.json, instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json), this record carries no documented award, no stated drone model or output resolution, and no verifiable engagement/follower signal (both the subscriber-count and description checks above came back empty) -- only a plausible, unverified impression of a competent single-pass travel-drone clip from the title/thumbnail/genre alone, so it is scored at the pack's low end rather than assumed comparable to its contest-winning/broadcaster exemplars. These are NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.73, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.74, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python3` in this session. `default_weights()` called with no arguments resolved to weights_version='default-v2' (sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25 each) -- this repo's current module default, matching what the-nature-project-horseshoe-bend.json's notes also found and diverging from the pack's oldest 10 records' notes, which describe 'default-v1' (1/3 each, composition=0.0). This does not change the arithmetic outcome here: `compute_composite_score` skips any signal whose score is None regardless of its weight and renormalizes by the weight actually applied, so with composition=None the three non-composition signals remain in equal proportion under either profile, reducing to the plain arithmetic mean of sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness. The call returned composite_score=0.73 exactly, matching (0.73+0.72+0.74)/3 exactly -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its inputs are estimates rather than measurements.
